text.title: Lessons Learned Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding Lessons Learned. Each pairs a binary check and a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Observation log entries MUST be captured in-flight, not batched at project close. Each entry has date, observer, signal, hypothesis.

rationale.source: Reason, Managing the Risks of Organizational Accidents

rule.rationale: Post-hoc retros lose ~40% of observations to memory decay; continuous logging preserves the contemporaneous signal.

rule.statement: Post-mortem MUST be blameless: focus on systems and processes, name no individuals. Personal mistakes describe context, not character.
rule.rationale: Blame-laden post-mortems destroy psychological safety; future incidents go unreported; learning collapses.

rule.statement: Every lesson translated into an action item MUST have a named owner, due date, and acceptance criterion. Orphan actions forbidden.
rule.rationale: Action items without owner/due rot in the backlog; the lesson dies with the retro.

rule.statement: Post-mortem report MUST be published cross-team in a searchable archive within 2 weeks of the milestone.
rule.rationale: Locked retros prevent cross-pollination; same mistake repeats across portfolio.

rule.statement: If the same lesson surfaces ≥2 times across projects, it MUST trigger a SOP / methodology update, not another retro entry.
rule.rationale: Recurring lessons indicate the lesson hasn't propagated to procedure; the rule forces structural fix instead of repeated recognition.

rule.statement: If preconditions for Lessons Learned fail (see Skip-If list in AGENTS.md), the operator MUST skip this methodology and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating an output.
rule.rationale: Applying Lessons Learned outside its envelope produces false-precision artefacts. Explicit skip preserves epistemic discipline.
